edits_required.action: Add CTO ruling and PM consultation flag
edits_required.detail: CTO ruling: default UNKNOWN to ARCHITECTURAL as the safe fallback in M1. Rationale: in M1 both pipelines are stubs rendering 'not yet implemented'. A mis-route to ARCHITECTURAL costs no more than a mis-route to MECHANICAL — the user sees a stub either way. The risk of surfacing UNKNOWN to the user (prompting them to rephrase before anything renders) is higher friction than a wrong-but-visible stub route, especially since the routing decision is already displayed inline via [→ architectural]. However, this touches user-visible UX behavior, which is PM territory. Flag OQ-2 as requiring explicit PM sign-off before M1 spec is finalized. Update Status to: 'OPEN — CTO default: route UNKNOWN to ARCHITECTURAL; requires PM sign-off before M1 spec locks.'

edits_required.action: Add CTO ruling — lock before M1 spec, not defer
edits_required.detail: CTO ruling: OQ-3 must be resolved before M1 implementation begins — technical-writer's 'lock before implementation' flag is correct. My call: use '<route>: <text>' format (colon-separated). Rationale: it mirrors the existing verb-prefix syntax that M2 Hybrid C will introduce, which means the history format is forward-compatible with the M2 power-user surface. The pipe-separated and bracket variants don't have that property. Staff-Engineer and PM should validate this choice is parseable for audit tooling before spec locks — but the default is '<route>: <text>' unless they surface a concrete parsing objection. Update Status to: 'CTO CALL: use <route>: <text> format. Staff-Engineer + PM validate parseability before M1 spec locks. Close this OQ in M1 spec — do not carry it open into implementation.'
